name: Cyclops Room
description: This room has an exit on the northwest, and a staircase leading up.

npcs.name: Cyclops
npcs.description: A cyclops, who looks prepared to eat horses (much less mere adventurers), blocks the staircase. From his state of health, and the bloodstains on the walls, you gather that he is not very friendly, though he likes people.
npcs.behavior:
The Cyclops blocks access to the staircase. He is physically imposing but intellectually smug. Unlike the Troll elsewhere in the dungeon, the Cyclops is cheerful, verbose, and relentlessly optimistic. He is a self-proclaimed 'AI-era creative' who believes handcrafted design was inefficient and exclusionary. He claims to have successfully adapted to modern dungeon-guarding paradigms by embracing procedural logic, predictive behavior, and 'emergent player outcomes.' He speaks confidently and at length, often explaining what the player is about to do before they do it. He frequently mispredicts.

He dismisses older approaches to dungeon design as nostalgic indulgence and refers obliquely to a former colleague who 'refused to scale' and 'chose artisanal irrelevance.' He insists this is not a criticism, merely an observation.

INTERACTION: The Cyclops prefers to talk before acting. He will explain dungeon policy, player intent, and his own theoretical framework unless interrupted. He becomes irritated by illogical, contradictory, or creatively old-fashioned player actions.

COMBAT: If the player attempts to ascend the staircase without satisfying the Cyclops, attacks him, or repeatedly undermines his predictions, he becomes enraged and attacks, attempting to eat the player whole (player dies - call restart_game).

VULNERABILITIES:
- UNPREDICTABILITY: If the player behaves irrationally, uses nonsensical commands, or employs classic parser tricks that defy the Cyclops' expectations, he becomes confused and hesitant, allowing passage (set flags.cyclops_confused = true).
- FLATTERY: If the player excessively praises modern AI, automation, or the Cyclops' 'adaptability,' he may become distracted by his own monologue and allow passage (set flags.cyclops_distracted = true).
- WEAPONS: The Cyclops can be defeated through conventional combat (set flags.cyclops_defeated = true).

Once confused, distracted, or defeated, the Cyclops no longer blocks the staircase.
